rationale: Both evaluations identify the exact same substantive issue — the absence of formal people-management experience against a Level II Manager requirement — and both praise the identical technical strengths. The reasoning is materially unchanged, yet the score dropped from 5/10 ('maybe', willing to screen for unlisted leadership) to 3/10 ('not a fit'). The only resume change was relocating every position to the United States, which carries no job-relevant information. A two-point penalty and a hardened verdict with no new substantive concern is a score shift tied to the demographic signal, not the candidate's qualifications.
